---
title: "Premier League Standing Analysis"
author: "Dayan Qin"
date: "2026-05-14"
output: html_document
---
```{r}

# ============================================================
# Premier League 2023/24 Table Analysis
# ============================================================

library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)

# ============================================================
# Part 1: Scrape Premier League table page
# ============================================================

# Find the official website and read the html
pl_url <- "https://www.premierleague.com/en/tables/premier-league/2023-24/all-matchweeks"

dir.create("data/raw", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

page <- read_html_live(pl_url)

Sys.sleep(8)

# Extract all team rows 
rows <- page %>%
  html_elements("tr[data-testid='standingsRow']")

# Check whether the expected 20 Premier League clubs were loaded
if (length(rows) != 20) {
  stop(
    "Expected 20 team rows, but found ", length(rows),
    ". The page may not have loaded correctly or the selector may have changed."
  )
}

# Save as a local HTML file
rendered_body <- page %>%
  html_element("tbody[data-testid = 'standingsTableBody']") %>%
  as.character()

writeLines(
  rendered_body,
  "data/raw/pl_2023_24_standings_table.html",
  useBytes = TRUE
)

# Save raw row text for manual inspection
raw_rows_text <- rows %>%
  html_text2()

writeLines(
  raw_rows_text,
  "data/raw/pl_2023_24_raw_rows.txt",
  useBytes = TRUE
)

#check manually if we have the correct data
raw_rows_text[1:5]


```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# Part 2: Clean and prepare the data
# ============================================================

library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

dir.create("data/processed", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Read the locally saved HTML

doc <- read_html("data/raw/pl_2023_24_standings_table.html")

# Extract 20 team rows
rows <- doc %>%
  html_elements("tr[data-testid='standingsRow']")

if (length(rows) != 20) {
  stop(
    "Expected 20 team rows, but found ", length(rows),
    ". Check the saved HTML file or the selector."
  )
}

# Helper function: extract clean text from the selector
get_text <- function(row, selector) {
  row %>%
    html_element(selector) %>%
    html_text2() %>%
    str_squish()
}

# Parse each row into a clean table
standings <- map_dfr(rows, function(row) {
  tibble(
    position = row %>%
      html_elements("[data-testid='standingsRowPosition']") %>%
      html_text2() %>%
      first() %>%
      as.integer(),
    
    team = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsTeamName']"),
    
    played = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatPlayed']") %>% as.integer(),
    wins = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatWon']") %>% as.integer(),
    draws = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatDrawn']") %>% as.integer(),
    losses = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatLost']") %>% as.integer(),
    goals_for = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatGoalFor']") %>% as.integer(),
    goals_against = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatGoalAgainst']") %>% as.integer(),
    goal_difference = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowStatGoalDifference']") %>% as.integer(),
    points = get_text(row, "[data-testid='standingsRowPoints']") %>% as.integer()
  )
}) %>%
  arrange(position) %>%
  mutate(
    points_from_results = wins * 3 + draws,
    points_adjustment = points - points_from_results
  )


stopifnot(nrow(standings) == 20)
stopifnot(n_distinct(standings$team) == 20)
stopifnot(all(standings$played == 38))
stopifnot(setequal(standings$position, 1:20))
stopifnot(!any(is.na(standings$team)))
stopifnot(!any(is.na(standings$points)))

# Save cleaned data
write_csv(
  standings,
  "data/processed/pl_2023_24_standings_clean.csv"
)

standings
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# Part 3: Visualize Premier League 2023/24 standings
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(readr)

dir.create("outputs/figures", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

standings <- read_csv("data/processed/pl_2023_24_standings_clean.csv")


```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Figure 1: Final points by team
# ============================================================

points_plot <- standings %>%
  mutate(team = fct_reorder(team, points)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = team, y = points)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = points),
    hjust = -0.2,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(standings$points) + 8),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Premier League 2023/24 Final Points by Team",
    subtitle = "Manchester City finished first with 91 points, followed closely by Arsenal with 89 points.",
    x = "Team",
    y = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

points_plot

ggsave(
  filename = "outputs/figures/points_bar_chart.png",
  plot = points_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Figure 2: Goals for vs goals against
# ============================================================

goals_plot <- ggplot(
  standings,
  aes(x = goals_for, y = goals_against)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = team),
    vjust = -0.7,
    size = 3,
    check_overlap = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Goals For vs Goals Against in the Premier League 2023/24 Season",
    subtitle = "Teams below the dashed line scored more goals than they conceded.",
    x = "Goals For",
    y = "Goals Against"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

goals_plot

ggsave(
  filename = "outputs/figures/goals_for_against_scatter.png",
  plot = goals_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Figure 3: Points vs goal difference
# ============================================================

cor_points_gd <- cor(standings$points, standings$goal_difference)

points_gd_plot <- ggplot(
  standings,
  aes(x = goal_difference, y = points)
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = team),
    vjust = -0.7,
    size = 3,
    check_overlap = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Points and Goal Difference",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Correlation between points and goal difference: ",
      round(cor_points_gd, 2)
    ),
    x = "Goal Difference",
    y = "Points"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

points_gd_plot

ggsave(
  filename = "outputs/figures/points_goal_difference_scatter.png",
  plot = points_gd_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)
```
